# Freest — setup guide for AI agents

> Freest is a free, fully offline voice-dictation app for the macOS menu bar.
> Hold the hotkey ⌃⌥D (Control-Option-D), speak, release, and Freest transcribes
> your speech on-device and pastes the text at the cursor. No accounts, no cloud,
> no API keys. Open source under Apache-2.0.
>
> This file is written for an AI coding agent to read and execute. Follow the
> steps in order. Everything is local to the user's Mac; the only network use is
> a one-time speech-model download.

## Facts

- Repository: https://github.com/JerryChen1974/Freest
- Language/build: Swift 6, Swift Package Manager (SwiftPM).
- Platform: macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later. Apple Silicon is required ONLY for the
  optional "Apple Intelligence" refinement mode (macOS 26+); every other feature
  works on any supported Mac, Intel or Apple Silicon.
- Default dictation hotkey: Control-Option-D, written ⌃⌥D. Configurable in Settings.
- Default speech model: `base` (downloaded once, then used offline).
- License: Apache-2.0.

## Prerequisites to check first

1. Confirm macOS version is 14.0 or newer:
   `sw_vers -productVersion`
2. Confirm the Swift toolchain is present:
   `swift --version`
   - If missing, install the Xcode Command Line Tools: `xcode-select --install`
   - A full Xcode is NOT required to build or run the app. (It is only needed to
     run the unit test suite via `swift test`; `scripts/test.sh` handles the
     Command Line Tools case. Testing is optional for end users.)
3. Confirm `git` is available: `git --version`

## Setup steps

### Step 1 — Clone and build the app

```sh
git clone https://github.com/JerryChen1974/Freest.git
cd Freest
scripts/build-app.sh
```

`scripts/build-app.sh` compiles a release build, assembles `Freest.app`, and
ad-hoc code-signs it. On success it prints the path to `Freest.app` in the repo
root. If the script is not executable, run `chmod +x scripts/build-app.sh` first.

### Step 2 — Launch the app

```sh
open Freest.app
```

Freest runs as a menu-bar app (a small waveform icon in the top-right menu bar).
It intentionally has NO Dock icon and NO main window.

### Step 3 — Grant permissions (first launch only)

Freest needs two macOS permissions. It will prompt on first use:

- Microphone — so it can hear you. (System Settings ▸ Privacy & Security ▸ Microphone)
- Accessibility — so it can paste text at the cursor in other apps.
  (System Settings ▸ Privacy & Security ▸ Accessibility)

Guide the user to approve both. If a prompt was dismissed, they can enable Freest
manually in the panes above.

### Step 4 — Download the speech model (one time)

Open the menu-bar dropdown and choose "Download model (base)". This is the only
step that uses the network. When it finishes, the menu shows "Model ready: base"
and Freest is fully offline from then on.

### Step 5 — Verify dictation works

1. Focus any text field (e.g. open TextEdit and create a new document).
2. Hold **⌃⌥D** (Control-Option-D).
3. Say a short sentence, e.g. "testing one two three".
4. Release the keys.
5. Confirm the transcribed text is pasted at the cursor.

If text appears, setup is complete.

## Settings the user can change

- Dictation hotkey (default ⌃⌥D).
- Speech model: tiny / base / small / medium / large-v3 (bigger = more accurate,
  slower, larger download).
- Refinement mode:
  - Off — paste the raw transcription.
  - Tidy — local rule-based cleanup (trim, collapse spaces, sentence casing,
    terminal punctuation, drop leading filler words). Offline, no model.
  - Apple Intelligence — on-device LLM cleanup (macOS 26+, Apple Silicon);
    automatically falls back to Tidy where unavailable.
- Input device, paste-at-cursor, and press-Return-after-paste toggles.

## Troubleshooting

- Build fails with "no such module 'Testing'": that only affects `swift test`,
  not the app build. Use `scripts/test.sh`, or ignore — tests are optional.
- Hotkey does nothing: confirm Accessibility permission is granted, and that the
  model shows "ready" in the menu bar.
- "Model not ready": complete Step 4 (download the base model) before dictating.
- Nothing is pasted but the pill shows it heard you: check Accessibility trust.

## Success criteria

- `Freest.app` launches and shows a menu-bar icon.
- Microphone and Accessibility permissions are granted.
- The `base` model shows as ready.
- Holding ⌃⌥D, speaking, and releasing pastes text at the cursor.
